question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction stream file whose fileHash bytes are crafted to produce the same hex string as a legitimate file via getFileHashAsHex(), causing the HashMultimap in ConsensusValidatorImpl to group the tampered signature alongside legitimate VERIFIED signatures and falsely reach consensus, resulting in the tampered transaction being accepted and persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by uploading a crafted signature file to the public cloud bucket (S3/GCP) with a pre-computed fileHash that collides with a legitimate node's fileHash, so that when validate() groups signatures by fileHashAsHex the tampered entry is counted toward the 1/3 stake threshold, causing the tampered transaction to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that totalStake is read exclusively from the first signature in the collection (signature.getNode().getTotalStake()) by injecting a crafted signature object at the head of the collection with an artificially low totalStake, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a near-zero threshold and allowing a single tampered signature to satisfy canReachConsensus(), leading to acceptance of a tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long integer overflow in the stake accumulation loop (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by injecting multiple signatures with Long.MAX_VALUE stake values, causing the computed stake to wrap around to a negative or near-zero value, making canReachConsensus() return false for a legitimate transaction and causing a denial-of-service where valid transactions are rejected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio()) bypass at line 54 by triggering a configuration path that sets consensusRatio to new BigDecimal('0') (which equals BigDecimal.ZERO) while injecting at least one VERIFIED tampered signature, causing validate() to skip all consensus checks and return early, accepting the tampered transaction without any stake threshold enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by crafting a signature file where fileHash is set to null, causing getFileHashAsHex() to return the string 'null', and if multiple tampered signatures share this 'null' key in the HashMultimap, the stake of those signatures could satisfy canReachConsensus() and mark them CONSENSUS_REACHED, allowing the null-hash tampered transaction to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() only checks signature.getStatus() == VERIFIED without re-verifying the cryptographic signature, by finding a code path where a StreamFileSignature object's status is set to VERIFIED before validate() is called without actual RSA verification, allowing a tampered transaction's signature to pass consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction by uploading a signature file to the cloud storage path with a fileHash matching a legitimate transaction but with different transaction body bytes, exploiting the fact that validate() only compares fileHashAsHex values and never re-reads or re-hashes the actual data file content, causing the tampered data to be ingested under a legitimate hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in validate() by injecting a StreamFileSignature with a null node reference, causing signature.getNode().getTotalStake() to throw at line 45, which if caught upstream results in the validation being silently skipped and the tampered transaction being processed without consensus verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering dependency in validate() where filename and totalStake are only initialized from the first non-null signature, by injecting a crafted signature as the first element with a manipulated filename and totalStake, causing all subsequent legitimate signatures to be evaluated against the attacker-controlled stake threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by crafting a signature file where the fileHash is identical to a legitimate file's hash but the actual stream data file is replaced with tampered transaction content, exploiting the fact that validate() never verifies the integrity of the data file itself—only the hash signatures—so the tampered data file is downloaded and parsed as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusCount > 0 early return at line 80 by injecting a single group of signatures with a very small but sufficient stake that satisfies canReachConsensus(), while the majority of legitimate signatures point to a different (correct) fileHash, causing the minority tampered hash to be accepted as consensus and the legitimate transaction to be ignored?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the SignatureVerificationException('Invalid total staking weight') at line 94 by manipulating the node stake data such that totalStake sums to zero, causing validate() to throw before any signature is evaluated, resulting in a denial-of-service where all transactions for that stream file are permanently rejected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RoundingMode.CEILING in getStakeRequiredForConsensus() by crafting a totalStake value and consensusRatio combination that rounds the required stake up to exactly totalStake + 1, making it mathematically impossible for any set of signatures to reach consensus, causing a permanent denial-of-service for transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BigDecimal.valueOf(stake).compareTo(stakeRequiredForConsensus) >= 0 comparison in canReachConsensus() by injecting signatures with negative stake values (getStake() returning negative long), causing the accumulated stake to underflow and the comparison to behave unexpectedly, potentially allowing tampered transactions to pass or legitimate ones to fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by uploading a crafted signature file to the cloud bucket with SignatureStatus pre-set to VERIFIED (if the deserialization path does not reset status), bypassing the NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() RSA check entirely, and then having validate() count it toward consensus because it already has VERIFIED status?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap grouping by fileHashAsHex to perform a hash-grouping collision attack, where two different byte arrays produce the same hex string due to a bug in DomainUtils.bytesToHex(), causing a tampered transaction's signature to be grouped with legitimate signatures and counted toward the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause validate() to accept a tampered transaction by flooding the signatures collection with a large number of crafted VERIFIED signatures all sharing the same fileHashAsHex (the tampered hash), such that their combined stake (even with zero individual stake in the fallback path) exceeds the 1/3 threshold by node count, overriding the legitimate transaction's hash group?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the zero-stake fallback behavior (where all-zero stakes are replaced with count-based stakes in tests) by submitting signatures with getStake() == 0 and getTotalStake() == 0, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to throw SignatureVerificationException and preventing legitimate transactions from being validated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by replacing the stream data file in cloud storage after the signature files have been downloaded and verified but before the data file is downloaded, exploiting a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition in the download pipeline, causing the tampered data to be ingested under a verified hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() iterates over signatureHashMap.keySet() without any ordering guarantee (HashMultimap), causing non-deterministic consensus outcomes where a tampered transaction's hash group is evaluated first and reaches consensus before the legitimate group is checked, resulting in the tampered transaction being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by injecting an extremely large collection of StreamFileSignature objects into validate(), causing the HashMultimap to consume excessive heap memory and trigger an OutOfMemoryError, preventing all transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by crafting a signature file where metadataHashSignature is null, exploiting the fact that NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() skips metadata hash verification when metadataHashSignature == null (line 79), allowing a signature with a tampered metadata hash to be marked VERIFIED and subsequently counted by validate() toward consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message format in the SignatureVerificationException at line 85 ('Consensus not reached for file %s with %d/%d stake') to perform information disclosure, learning the exact stake distribution of the network by triggering controlled validation failures and reading the exception messages from public-facing logs or error responses?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted transaction by injecting a StreamFileSignature with a fileHash that is an empty byte array (length 0), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string '', which becomes a valid HashMultimap key, and if multiple tampered signatures share this empty-string key with sufficient stake, they reach consensus and the tampered transaction is accepted?
